contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 60
- Description: Places API cost optimizations: photo gate, Nearby Search coverage cache, status refresh sweep
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/98
  
  Details:
  Three follow-ups to the persistent Place Details cache (PR #97). (1) Crawl-first photo gate in hit_list_research_photo_review.py: cheap site-crawl runs first using same KEYWORD_PATTERNS as detect_circle_hosting_retailers.py; only if Hosts Circles populated or fresh crawl finds qualifying keywords does the expensive photo+Grok path proceed. ~10% pass rate expected → photo line item drops to ~10% of current. New --photo-gate=strict|off flag. (2) Nearby Search coverage cache in places_cache.py + discover_apothecaries_la_hit_list.py: cached_nearby_search wraps the Nearby endpoint, stores per-combo coverage records at coverage/nearby/<sanitized>.json in places-cache repo, re-runs on already-covered geography skip the live /1k call entirely. New --refresh-coverage flag. (3) refresh_places_cache_status.py: walks every cached record via GitHub Trees API, re-fetches Basic-tier fields only (legacy Places Basic Data SKU is free), merges fresh Basic fields without touching Contact/Atmosphere, writes back. Flags closures + NOT_FOUND. Item 4 (Places API New migration): investigated and DEFERRED — per-SKU pricing actually makes our typical Basic+Contact pattern more expensive (/1k vs /1k Legacy), not less. Tested: all four scripts compile, refresh sweep dry-run runs cleanly against the live cache.
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
- TDG Issued: 100.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
